title: aristida plumosa subsp. sahelica
intro: Aristida plumosa subsp. sahelica, commonly known as Sahel Aristida or Sahel Threeawn, is a type of perennial grass native to the Sahel region in Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Aristida plumosa subsp. sahelica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves and stems in water, or it can be used as a topical paste to apply directly to affected areas.
